category_title: Eighteen-year-old autistic upstander with literacy difficulties who becomes a victim of peer ridicule
minimal_context: The student is discussing an online incident with a pastoral staff member during a morning interview.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please tell me about the incident in the online chat group last night.

STUDENT: Posting that video was the wrong thing to do. It was unfair because he was picking on a little kid.

TUTOR: What did you do next?

STUDENT: I told him he was out of order. But then they started attacking me online.

TUTOR: How did the conversation continue?

STUDENT: I started trying to argue my corner, but instead of being one against one it was five pupils against one girl. They were ridiculing my spelling and judging my poor English skills. I couldn't argue coherently in written form, and it's really affecting my mental health.

options.A: Ignore the student due to her undesirable behavior during the morning interview about the incident.
options.B: Offer a frank description noting that while she is a talented artist, she is dyslexic and struggles with writing.
options.C: Provide clues and requests for clarification regarding the details of the online incident during the interview.
options.D: Allow the student to not be rushed, giving her space to stop, think, and start again.
